'''Jonathan Walker''' (born 20 February 1991) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}}.

He has previously played for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Darlington Point Roosters]].

==Background==
Walker was born in England.

==Club career==
Walker started his career with [[Castleford Tigers]]. In 2009, he was loaned out to [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]],<ref>{{cite web|title=Gateshead Thunder signings ready to impress|url=http://www.chroniclelive.co.uk/sport/other-sport/rugby-league/gateshead-thunder-signings-ready-impress-1449387|website=Evening Chronicle|accessdate=21 February 2016|date=9 February 2009}}</ref> where he made seven appearances. He made his début for Castleford against [[Wigan Warriors]] in round 16 of 2010's [[Super League XV]], coming off the interchange bench for his first game.

He joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] from [[Castleford Tigers]] in October 2013.<ref name="sky">{{cite web|title=Super League: Jonathan Walker leaves Castleford to join twin at Hull KR|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/8955896/super-league-jonathan-walker-leaves-castleford-to-join-twin-at-hull-kr|website=Sky Sports|accessdate=15 December 2014|date=4 October 2013}}</ref> He spent one season at the club before joining [[Leigh Centurions]]. He spent 2015 on loan at the [[London Broncos]].

Walker signed a two-year deal with the [[Bradford Bulls]] following the 2015 season.

==Representative career==
Walker has been capped 4 times for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]].

He played in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] tournament, scoring his first international try in the tournament's opening game against Wales. Walker also played in the [[2015 European Cup]] tournament.

==Personal life==
Jonathan is the twin brother of the late rugby league player [[Adam Walker (rugby league)|Adam Walker]].<ref name="sky" />
